properties.title: Natanz Nuclear Facility
properties.description: Iran's largest uranium enrichment facility. Struck by Israeli jets on Jun 13 (Operation Rising Lion) and again by a US B-2 bomber dropping 2 GBU-57A/B bunker-buster bombs on Jun 22 (Operation Midnight Hammer). Also hit by a Tomahawk missile from submarine USS Georgia. Experts confirmed the enrichment hall was seriously damaged.

properties.title: Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant
properties.description: Iran's most hardened nuclear site, buried 80–90m inside a mountain near Qom. Six B-2 bombers dropped twelve GBU-57A/B MOP bunker-busters on Jun 22 sequentially into two ventilation shafts. The IAEA's Rafael Grossi later confirmed 'almost all sensitive equipment' at the site was destroyed.

properties.title: Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center
properties.description: Iran's largest nuclear research complex, housing uranium conversion and fuel fabrication facilities. Struck by Israeli jets on Jun 13 and hit by Tomahawk cruise missiles from submarine USS Georgia on Jun 22 as part of Operation Midnight Hammer.

properties.title: Tehran — Air Defenses & IRGC Targets
properties.description: In the opening hours of Operation Rising Lion (Jun 13), Mossad operatives and commando forces used smuggled precision weapons and a covert drone base near Tehran to disable air defense systems. Surface-to-air missile launchers in central Tehran struck Jun 14. IRGC headquarters and leadership targets hit throughout the conflict.

properties.title: Tabriz Air Base (TAB-2)
properties.description: Iran's 2nd Tactical Air Base, housing the 21st, 22nd, and 23rd Tactical Fighter Squadrons flying MiG-29 and F-5 aircraft. Struck on Jun 13 with several buildings north of hangars destroyed and runway approaches cratered. The Amand missile base north of Tabriz — storing Ghadr medium-range ballistic missiles — was also struck on Jun 14 and Jun 17.

properties.title: Hamadan Air Base (Shahid Nojeh)
properties.description: Iran's 3rd Tactical Air Base, housing the 31st Tactical Fighter Squadron flying F-4 Phantoms. Struck on Jun 13 with an aircraft hangar and radar site destroyed. Satellite imagery confirmed damage to the hangar on the northeastern side of the runway.

properties.title: Kermanshah — Missile Bases
properties.description: Multiple strikes on missile infrastructure in Kermanshah province. The Bakhtaran (Panj Pelleh) underground missile base storing Qiam-1 and Fateh-110 ballistic missiles was struck Jun 13. An IRGC warehouse in the Anahita neighborhood was hit Jun 15. The 1st Combat Assault Group fuel depot at Kermanshah Airport was also targeted.

properties.title: Parchin Military Complex
properties.description: A major military research and production facility 20km southeast of Tehran, suspected of testing explosive assemblies for nuclear weapons. Struck during Operation Rising Lion as part of Israel's effort to neutralise military-industrial infrastructure near the capital.

properties.title: Arak Heavy Water Reactor
properties.description: The IR-40 heavy water reactor under construction at Arak. Targeted by Israeli and US strikes for its potential role in plutonium production. The unfinished reactor was struck as part of the broader campaign to set back Iran's nuclear programme.
properties.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies / AJC

properties.title: Mashhad — Air Base Strike
properties.description: The IDF's furthest eastward strike of the Twelve-Day War. An Artesh Air Force base in the Mashhad area of northeastern Khorasan Razavi Province was targeted to extend air superiority across Iran and prevent aircraft from threatening Israeli operations.

properties.title: Bushehr — Missile Launch Sites
properties.description: Short and medium-range ballistic missiles stored at the 6th Tactical Airbase in Bushehr province were struck on Jun 20. Several ballistic missile launch and storage sites were destroyed.

properties.title: Ahvaz — IRGC Missile Base
properties.description: The IRGC 64th al Hadid Missile and Artillery Group base in Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province was struck on Jun 21, targeting missile storage and launch infrastructure in the oil-rich southwestern province.

properties.title: Shiraz — IRGC Missile Sites
properties.description: Missile launch sites at an IRGC-owned area near the IRGC Imam Ali Training Command in Shiraz, Fars Province, struck Jun 21. Multiple ballistic missile launchers and storage facilities targeted.

properties.title: Iran → Israel: Ballistic Missile Barrages
properties.description: Iran launched over 550 ballistic missiles and 1,000+ drones toward Israel during the Twelve-Day War (Operation True Promise III). 90 missiles were launched on Day 1 alone. Israel's multilayered air defences achieved a 92% interception rate. Civilian areas in Tel Aviv, Bat Yam, and Rehovot were struck, killing 9 and wounding ~200.

properties.title: Iran → Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar
properties.description: Iran retaliated against the US strike by firing missiles at Al Udeid Air Base on Jun 23. Iran pre-warned both Qatar and the US of the timing and coordinates — the strike was widely seen as symbolic, intended to signal capability without escalating further. No US casualties were reported.

properties.title: Tehran — Khamenei Assassination & Leadership Strikes
properties.description: On Feb 28, 2026, the opening salvo of Operation Epic Fury (US) and Operation Roaring Lion (Israel) killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and several family members in Tehran. Subsequent strikes targeted Iran's military leadership, government facilities, and IRGC command infrastructure. Mojtaba Khamenei was selected as the new Supreme Leader.

properties.title: Natanz — Revisited (2026)
properties.description: Despite the 2025 strikes setting back Iran's nuclear programme, the IAEA reported in Feb 2026 that Iran had restarted enrichment at an undamaged underground facility at Isfahan. US and Israeli strikes in the 2026 war revisited Natanz and other nuclear infrastructure as part of a broader campaign targeting military assets and leadership.

properties.title: Iran → Gulf States (2026 Retaliation)
properties.description: Iran launched hundreds of drones and ballistic missiles at targets across Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Iraq in retaliation for the Feb 28 strikes. Oil refineries, civilian areas, US military bases, and energy infrastructure were targeted. QatarEnergy reported damage to a gas field estimated to take 3–5 years to repair. The Strait of Hormuz was declared closed to commercial shipping.
properties.source: House of Commons Library / Al Jazeera

properties.title: Iran → Naval Support Activity Bahrain
properties.description: Iran struck the US Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain as part of its retaliatory campaign targeting US military infrastructure across the Gulf. UK forces at the base were also affected. The UK deployed additional air defences to Bahrain in response.

properties.title: Iran → Iraq (US Bases)
properties.description: Iranian missiles struck US military positions in Iraq as part of the broader retaliatory campaign. Iraq's position as both a US base host and an Iranian ally made it the most contested ground — the Iraqi government sought to distance itself from both sides while managing strikes on its territory.
